papers.title_en: LLM-Guided Transportation Hub Capacity Planning with Textual Business Inputs
papers.evidence: LLM引导的运输枢纽容量规划优化建模
papers.tldr: 传统枢纽容量规划方法依赖定量输入，无法利用蕴含业务意图的文本描述。本文提出一个LLM引导的迭代框架，核心是链式思维推理：代理将每条自然语言业务指令（如“扩大亚特兰大枢纽”）解析为方向与幅度，形成决策表，从而生成新的容量方案。下层路由优化模型提供性能指标反馈，引导代理逐步逼近最优。在涉及13个枢纽的东南部货运网络中，该框架实现2.8%的最优性差距，远低于传统模型的11.0%。成果表明LLM能有效桥接定性业务洞察与运筹优化，拓展了AI辅助决策的边界。
papers.abstract_en: While traditional hub capacity planning models optimize effectively for quantitative inputs, they often fail to digest qualitative business context. We propose a novel framework where a large language model (LLM) agent iteratively proposes hub capacity decisions guided by natural-language business context descriptions. The key mechanism is a chain-of-thought reasoning protocol: the LLM constructs a structured decision table that maps each contextual item to specific capacity adjustments based on the implied direction and magnitude of changes. The new capacity decision is then validated through a feedback loop with an optimization model, which provides routing-based performance metrics to guide the agent's selection. On a real-world 13-hub freight network in the southeastern US, our framework achieves a 2.8% optimality gap relative to the hidden ground-truth, a significant improvement over the 11.0% gap produced by the traditional optimization model without textual business inputs. This demonstrates that LLMs can serve as a contextual bridge, integrating qualitative business insights into Operations Research workflows.

papers.title_en: OptiAgent: End-to-End Optimization Modeling via Multi-Agent Iterative Refinement
papers.authors: Adriana Laurindo Monteiro, Nayse Fagundes, Gabriel Mattos Langeloh, Gustavo de Oliveira Kanno, Priscila Louise Aguirre, Thiago Costa Rizuti da Rocha, Victor Leme Beltran
papers.evidence: 利用LLM进行端到端的运筹优化建模与代码生成
papers.tldr: 运筹问题通常需人工构建数学模型，效率低且易出错。本文提出OptiAgent，一种多智能体框架，通过迭代细化从自然语言描述自动生成求解器可用的数学公式和可执行代码。该框架在数学建模步骤引入专用智能体提取决策变量和约束，并采用多循环验证架构与四种反馈机制分别处理误解、结构缺陷、数学不一致和代码错误。在LP、MILP和NLP任务的四个基准上，三个达到最优性能，剩余一个极具竞争力。其模块化设计提升了建模透明度和可审计性。
papers.abstract_en: We propose OptiAgent, a multi-agent framework that, given a natural language description of an Operations Research problem, is able to output a solver-ready mathematical formulation as well as executable code. Our architecture prioritizes the mathematical modeling step, where dedicated agents extract structures, such as decision variables and constraints, enabling iterative self-correction. We introduce a novel multi-loop validation architecture with four specialized feedback mechanisms, each targeting a distinct failure mode such as misinterpretation, structural defects, mathematical inconsistencies, validation failures, and code errors. Alongside accuracy, our modular design improves the process of solving optimization problems by improving transparency, as each agent exposes its reasoning and feedback, making the full modeling process auditable. Our framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on 3 out of 4 benchmarks across LP, MILP, and Nonlinear Programming tasks, while remaining highly competitive on the remaining dataset.

papers.title_en: Better Together, in the Right Order: Classical-then-LLM Optimization for SE
papers.evidence: 先经典优化后LLM的SE任务优化
papers.tldr: 软件工程配置任务中，常将大语言模型与经典优化器结合，但经典优化器主导搜索。本文提出SNAP2方法，先用廉价经典学习器获得结果，再用大语言模型精调。在100多个软件工程任务上，SNAP2在85%任务中达到最佳性能，优于单独使用大语言模型（75%）和经典优化器主导的方法；同时使用约少30%的令牌，速度快1.4倍。结果表明，学习器与大语言模型的组合顺序至关重要，先经典后大语言模型更优。
papers.abstract_en: A growing body of work combines large language models (LLMs) with classical optimizers for software engineering (SE) configuration tasks. Often, the classical optimizer is in charge: it owns the search loop and calls the LLM only to assist in subroutines (e.g. to warm-start the first generation, propose a mutation, or stand in as a surrogate). We report that there is much value in the reverse approach: seeding an LLM with the results from a cheap classical learner.   We call this method SNAP2. Applied to over 100 SE tasks, it is the single best of all methods studied, reaching the top tier on 85% of tasks, ahead of the same LLM run alone (75%) and ahead of every method in which the classical optimizer retains control. It is also less expensive: relative to the LLM-alone method, it uses roughly 30% fewer tokens and runs 1.4x faster, since the classical setup performs the inexpensive work, and the LLM is invoked only to finish.   We conclude that it is unwise to study classical learners or LLMs in isolation: there is much value in combining the two, and in the order that combination is applied.

papers.title_en: Which Algorithm Specification Formats Help Language Models Implement Machine Learning Algorithms?
papers.evidence: 研究算法规范格式对LLM代码生成准确性的影响直接适用于优化算法实现
papers.tldr: 大型语言模型常被用于从论文中实现算法，但论文表述的格式会影响首次实现的准确性。本研究对比了普通散文、LaTeX伪代码、PDF提取伪代码、Markdown、YAML、JSON和Python存根等7种格式，在5个ML任务、3个模型和4种设置下生成4020个实现。发现LaTeX伪代码在核心信息设置下效果最佳，但代码存根并未一致提升正确性。结论建议作者明确陈述接口、计算步骤和边界情况，而非依赖特定格式。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to implement algorithms from research manuscripts, but papers often leave implementation choices implicit. This study examines how the written format of an algorithm specification affects first-pass LLM implementation accuracy. We compare ordinary prose, LaTeX algorithm-style pseudocode, PDF-like extracted pseudocode, Markdown fields, YAML-like specifications, JSON-like specifications, and Python code stubs across five machine learning tasks, three models, and four experimental settings, yielding 4,020 generated implementations. Hidden tests evaluate details that often determine correctness, including tie-breaking, array shapes, numerical rules, return structures, and invalid-input behavior. Under the core-information setting, LaTeX algorithm-style pseudocode has the largest average format effect, with YAML-like specifications and ordinary prose close behind. Under complete information, GPT-5.4 mini shows no format differences in the matched comparisons, whereas Gemma 3 4B and Llama 3.2 3B still do. Code stubs do not consistently improve correctness despite specifying the function signature. The results support a writing recommendation: authors should state the interface, computation steps, numerical rules, and boundary-case behavior explicitly, instead of relying on a particular surface format to carry those details.

papers.title_en: Harness-Aware Self-Evolving: Co-Evolving Model Weights, Harness, and Task Solutions
papers.authors: Haochen Luo, Yi Huang, Sichun Luo, Fengyuan Liu, Lei Li, Zefa Hu, Junlan Feng, Qi Liu
papers.evidence: 强化学习框架自动生成优化问题解决方案
papers.tldr: 现有自进化框架通常固定外部组件，仅优化任务解，限制了性能提升。本文提出HASE，一个智能体强化学习框架，允许同一模型在多回合动作空间中生成任务解或编辑harness组件。在文本分类任务中，Qwen3-8B模型匹配了GPT-OSS-120B+Claude Code的性能，并在alpha因子挖掘和圆填充算法发现中取得超越或SOTA结果。HASE通过统一智能体过程实现harness与任务解的协同进化，显著提升了整体性能。
papers.abstract_en: Self-evolving frameworks usually optimize task solutions while treating the surrounding harness as fixed. We introduce Harness-Aware Self-Evolving (HASE), an agentic reinforcement-learning framework in which a single model can generate task solutions or edit selected harness components in a multi-turn action space. HASE enables a single Qwen3-8B model to match the text-classification performance of a GPT-OSS-120B model that uses Claude Code as the harness proposer. In alpha factor mining, HASE outperforms the reported GPT-OSS-120B baseline. HASE also repairs imperfect evaluation components and converges to state-of-the-art performance in circle-packing algorithm discovery. These results show that HASE improves the harness and the solution through one unified agentic process.

papers.title_en: From Failing to Passing: Evolving Natural Language Prompt Optimization Rules for LLM Code Generation
papers.authors: Amal Akli, Melissa Akli, Cedric Richter, Mike Papadakis, Yves Le Traon
papers.evidence: 针对LLM代码生成的提示优化
papers.tldr: 大语言模型对提示措辞高度敏感，这为改进代码生成提供了机会。本文提出搜索方法自动演化自然语言转换规则，并融合执行反馈构建双阶段修复流水线DUALFIX。在LiveCodeBench和APPS上，DUALFIX修复了10-30%的失败案例，其中12-17%是纯执行修复无法解决的；总体恢复30%的基线失败，修复量是Self-Fix的3-5倍。演化规则可跨问题、跨模型零样本迁移，展示了通用性和实用性。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models are known to be sensitive to prompt formulation. Even minor variations in wording can substantially degrade performance. This sensitivity reveals an opportunity: if prompt phrasing can harm performance, can it be used to improve it? To investigate this question, we introduce a search-based approach that identifies and evolves a set of natural language transformation rules with strong downstream effects on coding performance. We then propose DUALFIX, a staged repair pipeline that combines the evolved transformation rules with execution-feedback repair, addressing both specification-level and implementation-level failures. A key strength of our approach lies in its generality: the evolved rules are error-agnostic, reusable across problems, and transferable across models. We evaluate DUALFIX against execution-feedback repair baselines across three models on two challenging benchmarks, LiveCodeBench and APPS. Our results show that the evolved transformations fix from 10-30% of failing cases, including 12-17% of failures that execution-based repair alone cannot resolve. Overall, DualFix recovers up to 30% of baseline failures and fixes 3-5 times more failing cases than Self-Fix across all evaluated settings. Furthermore, we also show that rules evolved on one model transfer zero-shot to other models, outperforming execution-feedback repair without any re-optimization.
